section_a.question: Why do you like animals?
section_a.listening_and_speaking: Listening: Instructions to students and a conversation at the zoo. Speaking: Talk about your favourite animals
section_a.pronunciation: a /eɪ/, /æ/; ai /eɪ/; ay /eɪ/; al /ɔː/; aw /ɔː/; Unstressed words

section_b.question: How are animals part of our lives?
section_b.reading_and_writing: Reading: A post about a girl's favourite animal the elephant. Writing: Write a post about your favourite animal

section_a.question: What rules do we follow?
section_a.listening_and_speaking: Listening: Conversations and an announcement about school rules. Speaking: Talk about school rules
section_a.pronunciation: e /iː/, /e/; ea /iː/, /e/; ee /iː/; ear /ɪə/, /eə/; Sentence stress (1)

section_b.question: How can rules help us?
section_b.reading_and_writing: Reading: A letter of complaint and advice on rules. Writing: Write a letter to give advice
section_b.grammar: Imperatives; Modal verbs (can, have to, must)

section_a.question: How often do you do sport or exercise?
section_a.listening_and_speaking: Listening: Conversations about sports items and exercise habits. Speaking: Find the owners of sports items and ask about their exercise habits

section_b.question: How is exercise good for us?
section_b.reading_and_writing: Reading: Teenagers accounts of their favourite ways to keep fit. Writing: Write about your favourite way to keep fit

section_a.question: What do we like to eat?
section_a.listening_and_speaking: Listening: Conversations about meals and food preferences. Speaking: Order food and talk about preferences
section_a.pronunciation: o /əʊ/, /ɒ/, /ʌ/; oo /uː/, /ʊ/; ou /aʊ/; ow /aʊ/, /əʊ/; Intonation in alternative questions

section_b.question: How do we make healthy eating choices?
section_b.reading_and_writing: Reading: A newsletter article about improving eating habits. Writing: Write about your eating habits menu
section_b.grammar: Alternative questions; Countable and uncountable nouns

section_a.question: What are you doing right now?
section_a.listening_and_speaking: Listening: Phone conversations to invite friends to do something. Speaking: Have a phone conversation to find out what others are doing

section_b.question: How do we share our lives with others?
section_b.reading_and_writing: Reading: Descriptions of what people around the world are doing at the same time. Writing: Write a description of people's activities

units.title: Rain or Shine
units.essential_question: How does the weather affect us?

section_a.question: What's the weather like?
section_a.listening_and_speaking: Listening: A weather report and a conversation about weather and activities. Speaking: Talk about the weather and activities in different places
section_a.pronunciation: ar /ɑː/; er /ɜː/; ir /ɜː/; or /ɔː/, /ɜː/; ur /ɜː/; Rhythm

section_b.question: How do we feel about the weather?
section_b.reading_and_writing: Reading: Posts about climbing Mount Huangshan. Writing: Write a description of weather and activities report

units.title: A Day to Remember

section_a.question: What was your special day like?
section_a.listening_and_speaking: Listening: Conversations about special days. Speaking: Talk about a school trip
section_a.pronunciation: c /k/, /s/; ck /k/; g /ɡ/, /dʒ/; x /ks/; Contractions in negative forms

section_b.question: What did you learn on that special day?
section_b.reading_and_writing: Reading: A diary entry about a school trip to a farm. Writing: Write a diary entry about a school trip

units.project: Write a chain story about a memorable day

units.title: Once upon a Time

section_a.question: What are your favourite stories?
section_a.listening_and_speaking: Listening: Conversations which tell stories. Speaking: Retell a story
section_a.pronunciation: ch /tʃ/; ph /f/; sh /ʃ/; th /θ/, /ð/; wh /w/, /h/; Assimilation

section_b.question: What can stories teach us?
section_b.reading_and_writing: Reading: The story The Ugly Duckling. Writing: Write an ending for a story

units.project: Create and perform a short play
